summary: Iteration 3 — PWA + Compliance + Adaptive Engine + Admin Tools + AI new modes. Backend: 71/71 pytest tests PASSED (100%) — 49 regression + 22 new (PWA static asset checks, Compliance CRUD/quiz/expiry, Adaptive heatmap/locked-labs, Admin sites/inventory/checkout/return, AI nec_lookup + blueprint modes). Frontend: All five new flows verified end-to-end via Playwright on the public preview URL — manifest+sw.js+PWA meta tags load correctly, /adaptive shows 8 heatmap cells with hot/warm/cold coloring + 4 recommendations + battery-inverter-build locked, /compliance lists 4 modules and LOTO quiz section renders, /admin/tools admin-only access (student is redirected to /dashboard) with all 3 tabs (Sites=10, Inventory=16 items, Checkout=working selects/table), AI Tutor exposes NEC Lookup and Blueprint Reader modes. No critical bugs.

minor.issue: Returns {ok:true} only — does not echo the updated checkout doc with status='returned'. Frontend has to re-fetch the list to see status. Minor UX, not a bug.

heatmap is returned as a dict keyed by competency slug rather than an array. Functionally fine and the frontend renders correctly via Object.entries, but inconsistent with typical 'list' shapes — consider normalising to a list of {key,name,level,points,labs_passed} for easier client iteration.

issues: React hydration console warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — caused by the Emergent instrumentation script injecting <span data-ve-dynamic='true' style={display:contents}> into <option> elements rendered from a .map(). Browsers tolerate it (selects still work), but it spams the dev console. Suggest wrapping option text in a plain string, e.g. {`${i.sku} — ${i.name} (${i.quantity_available})`}, with no nested expression children, to avoid the wrapper span.
Spec said 6 mode buttons; UI now renders 7. Both 'NEC Lookup' (mode-nec_lookup) and 'Blueprint Reader' (mode-blueprint) are present and switching works. Just confirm the 7th is intentional.

action_items: (Optional) Make /api/admin/checkout/{id}/return return the updated checkout document with status='returned' instead of {ok:true}.
(Optional) Normalise /api/adaptive/me 'heatmap' to a list to match typical REST shapes.
(Optional) Eliminate the <span>-in-<option> hydration warning on /admin/tools by simplifying option children to a single string expression.
(Optional) Confirm the 7th AI mode button is intentional or trim back to the 6 advertised in the spec.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1517 lines — has grown another 320 lines this iteration. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/{compliance,adaptive,admin_tools,ai}.py before further additions.

Adaptive locked-labs prereqs are hardcoded inside /api/adaptive/me (battery-inverter-build → solar-charge-controller, loto-real-equipment → loto-scenario). For maintainability, move prereqs into the lab document itself or a small config map.

Compliance quiz uses a separate compliance_modules collection — good separation. Verify expiry recompute on resubmit (currently quiz POST resets expires_at every time the student passes — confirm that is intentional).
PWA sw.js is minimal (cache-first /, /manifest.json). Good as a baseline; consider adding a NETWORK_TIMEOUT and skipWaiting/clientsClaim for faster updates.
seed_data_creation: test_create_site_admin creates a 'test-site-<rand>' site each run (left in DB — sites grid showed 10 entries, growing). test_checkout_flow creates and immediately returns a checkout, leaving inventory quantity unchanged. The student account also gets osha-10-electrical and loto-certification compliance modules marked completed and accumulates osha-10-awareness credential.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-3 added /app/backend/tests/test_iter3.py — 22 tests for PWA/Compliance/Adaptive/AdminTools/AINewModes/Regression. Run together: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Adaptive heatmap is a DICT keyed by competency slug (not a list). Admin checkout/return endpoint returns {ok:true} not the updated doc. Sidebar testid visibility check immediately post-login can race with auth context hydration (false negative on /dashboard); navigating to a child route like /adaptive resolves the timing — the sidebar items WERE present in the screenshot.
